title: After Initial Opsec Training Upon Arrival To The Command
source_filename: After Initial Opsec Training Upon Arrival To The Command.docx

qas.a: A process that is a systematic method used to identify, control, and protect critical information

qas.q: OPSEC?s most important characteristic is that:
The purpose of OPSEC is to:
qas.a: Reduce the vulnerability of US and multinational forces from successful adversary exploitation of critical information.

qas.q: An OPSEC indicator is defined as:
qas.a: Friendly detectable actions and open-source information that can be interpreted or pieced together by an adversary to derive critical information.

qas.q: After initial OPSEC training upon arrival to the command all personnel are required to:
The Joint COMSEC Monitoring Activity provides OPSEC assistance by:
qas.a: Monitoring unclassified government telephones and monitoring NIPR email traffic.

qas.q: The identification of critical information is a key part of the OPSEC process because:
qas.a:
** NOT **
• It focuses the OPSEC process on protecting all unclassified information.
• None of the answers are correct.

qas.q: Who has oversight of the OPSEC program?
qas.a:
** NOT **
• The EUCOM Directors and EUCOM Component Commanders.
• All answers are correct

qas.q: OPSEC countermeasures can be used to:
qas.a: Prevent the adversary from detecting an indicator and from exploiting a vulnerability.
Identifying, controlling, and protecting unclassified information that is associated with specific military operations and activities.

qas.q: Operations Security (OPSEC) defines Critical Information as:
OPSEC as a capability of Information Operations

qas.a: The adversary is capable of collecting critical information, correctly analyzing it, and then taking timely action.

** NOT **
• In the EUCOM J2/Intelligence Directorate.
